model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Iowa holds a slight analytical edge with superior KenPom (25 vs 36) and Torvik (26 vs 29) rankings, featuring significantly better offensive efficiency (rank 29 vs 69). Clemson's loss of starting center Carter Welling to a torn ACL weakens their interior defense and rebounding, which could be exploited by Iowa's balanced attack. While Clemson has strong defensive metrics (rank 20), the Hawkeyes' offensive firepower and slight betting favoritism (-1.5) give them the advantage in this closely matched 8/9 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Clemson vs Iowa South Region 8/9 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated team ratings, injury impact, and matchup dynamics for this evenly paired contest.
reasoningStep.evidence: Iowa ranks 25th in KenPom and 26th in Torvik vs Clemson's 36th and 29th respectively. Iowa's offensive efficiency (rank 29) significantly outpaces Clemson's (rank 69). Clemson lost starting center Carter Welling to a torn ACL in the ACC Tournament, weakening their interior presence. ESPN Analytics shows a near 50/50 split (49.5% Iowa, 50.5% Clemson). Betting markets favor Iowa by 1.5 points. Both teams have similar defensive profiles (Clemson rank 20, Iowa rank 35 in defensive efficiency).

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
